name: Eggplant, Lamb and Rice Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 Red Bell Pepper, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Cut eggplants in half lengthwise. Place the halves, cut-side down, in a roasting pan. Add water to a depth of 1/2 inch. Bake until tender, about 20-25 minutes. Set aside.
3. Reduce oven temperature to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. While the eggplant is roasting, heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground lamb and sauté, breaking up the meat with a wooden spoon, until browned, 3-5 minutes. Transfer to a colander and drain off fat. Set aside.
5. In a Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add onions and cook until golden, about 5 minutes. Add red peppers and garlic and cook for 2 minutes longer. Stir in tomatoes and their juice, oregano, thyme, cinnamon, and cloves. Simmer, breaking up tomatoes with a wooden spoon, until the mixture has thickened slightly, 3-5 minutes.
6. Scoop out eggplant flesh and chop coarsely. Stir rice, beef broth, chopped eggplant, and the reserved lamb into the tomato mixture. Bring to a simmer. Cover the pan and place it in the oven.
7. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the rice is tender and all the liquid has been absorbed. Season with salt and pepper. Dot with feta and serve.
